text_blocks.text: Immunodeficiencies occur when one or more
of the components of the immune system are
inactive. The ability of the immune system to
respond to pathogens is diminished in both the
young and the elderly, with immune responses
beginning to decline at around 50 years of
age due to immunosenescence. In developed
countries, obesity, alcoholism, and drug use are
common causes of poor immune function. However,
malnutrition is the most common cause of
lacking sufficient protein are associated with
the loss of the thymus at an early age through
genetic mutation or surgical removal results in
severe immunodeficiency and a high susceptibility
